title: Committee Meetings Show Meetings for the UN
display_title: Committee Meetings Show Meetings for the UN
wikitext:
[]: Committee Meetings: Show-Meetings for the U.N.?             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Mon Aug 03, 2009 3:25 am I've been doing a simultaneous viewing of Rebuild 1.01 and the corresponding series episodes. A Committee scene in the original is replaced by a Seele scene in Rebuild, wherein Seele talks about certain things very differently from how the Committee did. They don't seem to give a damn about budgets, for one. (I know that Rebuild =/= Series. That's not where I'm going with this.) This brought to the fore in my mind something that had been on the back burner for a while: could it be that, in the series, the early meetings between Gendo and the Committee are official and recorded, thus what they say is being heard by outside ears at least potentially, thus they are all actually putting on a show for the UN? I bring this up because, in the commentary project, I read some confusion as to why the Committeemen seem overly concerned with costs in that scene in ep2, and also strangely comment about 2I and the return of the Angels as being unexpected. This would explain those discrepancies. The "the Angels aren't coming back" idea is an official Committee position, too, as evidenced by the fact that a character called Chinese Guy knows about it: Episode 7 wrote: CHINESE GUY: Excuse me. since we're sharing the flight, do you mind if I sit here? CHINESE GUY: The revised budget for the sample collection passed quite easily, didn't it? IKARI: The Committee's main concern is for their own survival. They aren't stingy when their own lives are at stake. CHINESE GUY: After all, the basis of their argument was that no more Angels would appear.

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Mon Aug 03, 2009 3:43 am I think this early behavior is meant to fool the viewers and not spoil anything for them. It's the early episodes, and disclosing anything at this moment would make bad pacing, not to mention, kill the story.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Mon Aug 03, 2009 3:51 am That would be the dramatic purpose, obviously; but of course, I'm talking about in-universe reasons for their behavior. The reason it keeps nagging at me that this may be the case is those dang grins they have throughout that whole first Committee meaning. Hm... Better check to see if they also have those grins in ep14, though.

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Mon Aug 03, 2009 3:55 am Did they all had grins all the time? The USA member grinned when he made the sarcastic remark to Gendo about letting his son controlling the EVA-01, but that's understandable. Also, grinning all the time makes cheesy villains.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Mon Aug 03, 2009 5:39 am Only one I remember grinning was the hook nosed one

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Mon Aug 03, 2009 8:14 am I don't think all of them were grinning, but the idea is a good one, nevertheless. The committee considers the loss of one-third of the huge UN fleet in Episode 08 to be pretty trivial. That's not what a committee concerned about bankrupting smaller nations would think like if they were serious about what they meant. However, what Keel says to Gendo at the very end of the first meeting would appear to be off the record.

[]:             Quote Post by Agent_Koopa » Tue Aug 04, 2009 1:44 pm It's an excellent suggestion. One thing, though. What is the UN to make of a "Human Instrumentality Committee"? What is their ostensible purpose?

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Tue Aug 04, 2009 3:53 pm Agent_Koopa wrote: It's an excellent suggestion. One thing, though. What is the UN to make of a "Human Instrumentality Committee"? What is their ostensible purpose?

[]:             Quote Post by VoidEater » Tue Aug 04, 2009 4:06 pm Haven't paid it much mind, just always assumed Committee was a public body used to finnel funding to Seele (more like a private Board of Directors) and its projects.

[]:             Quote Post by Sharaz Destler » Tue Aug 04, 2009 5:37 pm RE: Monk Ed Your theory makes sense to me, because I was just thinking about how the SEELE committee start out as real-time holographs (the show meetings for the UN) and later on take the form of the Black Monoliths (when the REAL business is going down).

[]:             Quote Post by Themaninblack » Wed Aug 05, 2009 6:19 am Xard wrote: Only one I remember grinning was the hook nosed one
